50. Examining whether relaxation of cut off marks can be granted to the 
appellant, reliance may be placed to Taniya Malik v. High Court of 
Delhi,40 wherein it was held as under:
‘Merely by the fact that some more posts were advertised 
and they are lying vacant, it could not have been a ground 
to relax the minimum marks for interview after the interview 
has already been held. It would not have been appropriate 
to do so and the High Court has objected to relaxation of 
minimum passing marks in viva voce examination in its
